note: Stands on a large flat pedestrian square (Pariser Platz) with smooth paving; fully step-free to approach and photograph. Easy for everyone.
Free to visit with a lift to the roof terrace and an accessible ramp spiralling up inside the glass dome; pre-registration required. Fully wheelchair accessible.
The museums have lifts and accessible entrances with free disabled entry; some are under renovation. The island's approaches have a few cobbles but routes are step-free.
A flat, open-air 1.3km stretch of the Wall along a paved riverside path; entirely step-free and easy to wheel or push along.

place: Memorial to the Murdered Jews of Europe
note: The field of stelae has an undulating stone-paved ground that is step-free but gently uneven; the underground information centre is fully accessible by lift. Free.
Enormous flat central park with paved and firm paths, benches and cafes; excellent for strollers, wheelchairs and a slow senior stroll.
A lift carries visitors to the observation deck; the deck and bar are accessible, though space is limited at peak times. Book ahead.
Ground-floor State Rooms have an accessible route but upper floors and some historic rooms have stairs; the gardens are flat and easy. Partly accessible.
An open, flat street-level site; easy to reach and view, though touristy and crowded. The associated museum has some stairs.
A grand flat square with smooth paving between the two cathedrals and concert hall; step-free to cross and enjoy, some steps up to the buildings.
Largely flat paved paths throughout with accessible toilets and rentable wheelchairs; a classic easy family day, a few gentle slopes.
